meaning: vt.完成,使达到极点adj.圆满的,至上的,完美的
examples: Findings from several studies on corporate mergers and acquisitions during the 1970's and 1980's raise questions about why firms initiate and consummate such transactions.
He acted the part with consummate skill.
Creating more wealth isn't the driver This consummate dealmaker is motivated by unfinished business.
He remains the consummate amateur, drawing his authority from the peculiar independence of his work.
